finding: Self-protection goals invert the affective consequences of upward counterfactual thinking: instead of generating negative affect and increased motivation, exculpatory counterfactuals maintain self-esteem while reducing future preparation effort when the explanation blames unstable external factors.

People judge risky outcomes as more likely when spontaneously imagining negative consequences is easier (high accessibility), even absent rational basis; this availability heuristic operates automatically unless deliberative reasoning explicitly overrides it.

Expressing relational vulnerability triggers beliefs that partners view the self as insecure, which activates prototypes of cautious inauthentic responding; these authenticity doubts then persistently underestimate partners' true positive regard, perpetuating the insecurity that generated the expression.

Red color automatically increases male attractiveness-and-sexual-desire perception of women independent of conscious awareness, operating equally across different color contrasts and context types, mediated by color-sex semantic associations rather than general perceptual arousal.

Marital decline and dissolution reflect dynamic equilibrium between competing forces (attractions, barriers, alternatives, contextual stress, adaptation capacity) whose relative balance shifts over time; different couples may reach divorce at different durations despite similar initial dissatisfaction.

Personality traits predict subjective well-being through both temperamental pathways (neuroticism→negative affect, extraversion→positive affect) and instrumental pathways (conscientious/agreeable behavior shapes favorable life outcomes); overall effect is moderate but robust across trait diversity.
